preamble: The raising of back-rounded nuclei in diphthongs like 'au' leads toward 'ou' or higher configurations, often seen in systematic vowel shifts.

examples.note: Part of the Great Vowel Shift where Middle English long [ɔ:] raised to [o:] and later diphthongized into [ou].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
